name: fresh_packet_silently_dropped
code:
proof fn phi_1_fresh_packet_silently_dropped(pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState, pkt: Packet, out: Set<Packet>, ack: Packet)
    requires
        receive_packet(pre, post, pkt, out, ack),
        pkt.msg is Message,
        pre.received_packet is None,
        SingleDelivery::new_single_message(pre.sd, pkt),
    ensures
        post.received_packet is None,
{
}
reason: If provable, fresh messages are silently dropped even when the buffer is empty, violating reliable delivery

name: new_message_implies_should_ack
code:
proof fn phi_2_new_message_implies_should_ack(pre: AbstractHostState, pkt: Packet)
    requires
        pkt.msg is Message,
        SingleDelivery::new_single_message(pre.sd, pkt),
    ensures
        SingleDelivery::should_ack_single_message(pre.sd, pkt),
{
}
reason: new_single_message (seqno == last+1) and should_ack (seqno <= last) must be mutually exclusive; overlap collapses sequencing

name: receive_ack_always_noop
code:
proof fn phi_3_receive_ack_always_noop(pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState, pkt: Packet, out: Set<Packet>, ack: Packet)
    requires
        receive_packet(pre, post, pkt, out, ack),
        pkt.msg is Ack,
    ensures
        post == pre,
{
}
reason: If ack processing never advances send-state, retransmission queues grow without bound and acked data is never freed

name: ack_routed_wrong_direction
code:
proof fn phi_4_ack_routed_wrong_direction(pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState, pkt: Packet, out: Set<Packet>, ack: Packet)
    requires
        receive_packet(pre, post, pkt, out, ack),
        pkt.msg is Message,
        !out.is_empty(),
    ensures
        ack.dst == pkt.dst,
{
}
reason: Acks must go back to the sender (pkt.src); routing to pkt.dst means the ack never reaches the originator

name: receive_never_emits_packets
code:
proof fn phi_5_receive_never_emits_packets(pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState, pkt: Packet, out: Set<Packet>, ack: Packet)
    requires
        receive_packet(pre, post, pkt, out, ack),
    ensures
        out == Set::<Packet>::empty(),
{
}
reason: If receive_packet never emits outbound packets, host_model_receive_packet can never send acks, breaking the reliable delivery protocol entirely
